bead.title: exec: shared reviewer + closure failure-case fixtures under cli/internal/agent/testdata
bead.description:
Pre-requisite for the three reviewer bugs (ddx-f7ae036f, ddx-f8a11202, ddx-738edf47) and the closure invariant (ddx-e30e60a9). Codex plan review suggested landing shared fixtures first so the three reviewer fixes are tested against real artifacts, not regex-guess-driven.

## Work

Capture real-world failure artifacts from the review-malfunction-report into cli/internal/agent/testdata/ (or equivalent location next to the reviewer code):

1. `reviewer-stream-approve-misextracted.jsonl` — a captured stream the reviewer emitted that ended with APPROVE but the current parser records as BLOCK. Target: drive ddx-f7ae036f's regex tests.
2. `reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl` — ~2MB synthetic reviewer response. Target: drive ddx-f8a11202's truncation + artifact-reference assertions.
3. `bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl` — a beads.jsonl row with a 1.46MB event body (mirrors the real corruption the reporter hit). Target: drive ddx-f8a11202's read-side scanner-buffer regression.
4. `bead-closed-no-evidence.jsonl` — a beads.jsonl row with status:closed and events:[]. Target: drive ddx-e30e60a9's invariant test.

Each fixture should include a short README noting its source incident, the expected pre-fix failure, and the expected post-fix behavior.

Not a product change; test-infrastructure groundwork only.
bead.acceptance:
(1) Four byte-frozen fixture files land under cli/internal/agent/testdata/reviewer/, captured from real on-disk artifacts (not synthesized):
  - reviewer-stream-approve-misextracted.jsonl — from .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl, citing source session id + offset in README.
  - reviewer-stream-oversized-body.jsonl — the real ~2MB reviewer stream behind the 1.46MB beads.jsonl row, citing source path in README.
  - bead-jsonl-oversized-line.jsonl — the actual corrupted beads.jsonl row, trimmed, citing source path + line number in README.
  - bead-closed-no-evidence.jsonl — axon-c5cc071a's closed-state row, citing source tracker path in README.
(2) Each fixture has a README header: source path, capture date, pre-fix failure mode, expected post-fix behavior.
(3) Fixtures load cleanly (syntactic/format validity only); behavior-specific failing tests live on the consumer beads (ddx-f7ae036f, ddx-f8a11202, ddx-738edf47, ddx-e30e60a9), not here.
(4) Fixtures pin to v0.6.0-era ddx-agent output. A v0.7.0 capture is filed as a one-line follow-up after ddx-e7254128 lands.
